description: Agilent (Keysight/HP) E3633A and E3634A single-output programmable DC power supplies. SCPI command set over GPIB and RS-232, supporting dual-range output setting (APPLy and low-level SOURce commands), voltage/current measurement, overvoltage (OVP) and overcurrent (OCP) protection, triggered output levels, front-panel display control, state storage, calibration and status reporting. E3633A: 8V/20A or 20V/10A ranges; E3634A: 25V/7A or 50V/4A ranges.

identity.description: Reads the power supply identification string. Returns four comma-separated fields: manufacturer, model number, a field always '0', and a revision code with three numbers (main processor, I/O processor, front-panel processor). For example: HEWLETT-PACKARD,E3633A,0,X.X-X.X-X.X

commands.name: apply
commands.description: Set the output voltage and current in a single command (combination of VOLTage and CURRent). Changes the output only if the programmed values are valid within the presently selected range. If only one parameter is given it is treated as the voltage setting. MIN selects 0 V / 0 A; MAX selects the highest values allowed for the selected range; DEF sets 0 V and the default current (20 A for E3633A, 7 A for E3634A).

parameters.name: voltage
parameters.range: 0 to 8.24 (8V range) or 0 to 20.60 (20V range) for E3633A; 0 to 25.75 (25V range) or 0 to 51.5 (50V range) for E3634A; or DEF|MIN|MAX

parameters.name: current
parameters.range: 0 to 20.60 (8V range) or 0 to 10.30 (20V range) for E3633A; 0 to 7.21 (25V range) or 0 to 4.12 (50V range) for E3634A; or DEF|MIN|MAX

commands.name: set_current
commands.description: Program the immediate current level at the output terminals, regardless of the presently selected output range. MIN = 0 A, MAX = highest current allowed for the selected range. UP/DOWN increase or decrease the level by the CURRent:STEP amount; exceeding the rated current causes execution error -222 (Data out of range). Query returns the present current level; CURR? MAX and CURR? MIN return the highest and lowest programmable levels for the selected range.

parameters.name: current
parameters.range: 0 to range maximum (e.g. 20.60 A E3633A 8V range); or MIN|MAX|UP|DOWN

commands.name: current_step
commands.description: Set the step size used by the CURRent UP and CURRent DOWN commands. Setting the step size to DEFault selects the minimum resolution (approx. 0.32 mA for E3633A, 0.13 mA for E3634A). At *RST the step size is the minimum resolution. Query returns the present step size; STEP? DEFault returns the minimum resolution in amps.

parameters.name: step
parameters.range: numeric value or DEFault (min resolution ~0.32 mA E3633A, ~0.13 mA E3634A)

commands.name: current_triggered
commands.description: Program the pending triggered current level: a stored value transferred to the output terminals when a trigger occurs. Not affected by subsequent CURRent commands. Query returns the triggered level (or the present CURRent level if none programmed); MAX/MIN return the highest and lowest programmable triggered levels.

commands.name: current_protection
commands.description: Set the current level at which the overcurrent protection (OCP) circuit trips. If the peak output current exceeds the OCP level the output current is programmed to zero and the Questionable Status 'OC' bit is set. Clear with CURR:PROT:CLE after removing the cause. Query returns the present OCP trip level; MAX/MIN return the maximum and minimum programmable trip levels.

parameters.name: current
parameters.range: programmable OCP trip level; or MIN|MAX

commands.name: current_protection_state
commands.description: Enable or disable the overcurrent protection (OCP) function. At *RST this value is set to ON. Query returns '0' (OFF) or '1' (ON).

commands.name: current_protection_tripped
commands.description: Query whether the overcurrent protection circuit is tripped. Returns '1' if tripped and not cleared, '0' if not tripped.

commands.name: current_protection_clear
commands.description: Clear the tripped overcurrent protection circuit. The OCP trip level remains unchanged. Before sending, lower the output current below the OCP trip point or raise the OCP trip level above the output setting; any external overcurrent condition must be removed first.

commands.name: set_voltage
commands.description: Program the immediate voltage level at the output terminals, regardless of the presently selected output range. MIN = 0 V, MAX = highest voltage allowed for the selected range. UP/DOWN increase or decrease the level by the VOLTage:STEP amount; exceeding the rated voltage causes execution error -222 (Data out of range). Query returns the present voltage level; VOLT? MAX and VOLT? MIN return the highest and lowest programmable levels for the selected range.

parameters.name: voltage
parameters.range: 0 to range maximum (e.g. 8.24 V E3633A 8V range); or MIN|MAX|UP|DOWN

commands.name: voltage_step
commands.description: Set the step size used by the VOLTage UP and VOLTage DOWN commands. Setting the step size to DEFault selects the minimum resolution (approx. 0.36 mV for E3633A, 0.95 mV for E3634A). At *RST the step size is the minimum resolution. Query returns the present step size; STEP? DEFault returns the minimum resolution in volts.

parameters.name: step
parameters.range: numeric value or DEFault (min resolution ~0.36 mV E3633A, ~0.95 mV E3634A)

commands.name: voltage_triggered
commands.description: Program the pending triggered voltage level: a stored value transferred to the output terminals when a trigger occurs. Not affected by subsequent VOLTage commands. Query returns the triggered level (or the present VOLTage level if none programmed); MAX/MIN return the highest and lowest programmable triggered levels.

commands.name: voltage_protection
commands.description: Set the voltage level at which the overvoltage protection (OVP) circuit trips. If the peak output voltage exceeds the OVP level the output is shorted by an internal SCR and the Questionable Status 'OV' bit is set. Clear with VOLT:PROT:CLE after removing the cause. Query returns the present OVP trip level; MAX/MIN return the maximum and minimum programmable trip levels.

parameters.name: voltage
parameters.range: programmable OVP trip level; or MIN|MAX

commands.name: voltage_protection_state
commands.description: Enable or disable the overvoltage protection (OVP) function. At *RST this value is set to ON. Query returns '0' (OFF) or '1' (ON).

commands.name: voltage_protection_tripped
commands.description: Query whether the overvoltage protection circuit is tripped. Returns '1' if tripped and not cleared, '0' if not tripped.

commands.name: voltage_protection_clear
commands.description: Clear the tripped overvoltage protection circuit. The OVP trip level remains unchanged. Before sending, lower the output voltage below the OVP trip point or raise the OVP trip level above the output setting; any external overvoltage condition must be removed first.

commands.name: voltage_range
commands.description: Select the output range to be programmed. For E3633A: P8V (or LOW) = 8V/20A range, P20V (or HIGH) = 20V/10A range. For E3634A: P25V (or LOW) = 25V/7A range, P50V (or HIGH) = 50V/4A range. At *RST the low range (P8V for E3633A, P25V for E3634A) is selected. Query returns the currently selected range identifier.

commands.name: measure_current
commands.description: Query the current measured across the current sense resistor inside the power supply.

commands.name: measure_voltage
commands.description: Query the voltage measured at the sense terminals of the power supply.

commands.name: initiate
commands.description: Initiate the trigger system. Completes one full trigger cycle when the trigger source is immediate, or initiates the trigger subsystem when the trigger source is bus.

commands.name: trigger_delay
commands.description: Set the time delay between detection of an event on the trigger source and the start of the corresponding output change. Valid only for the bus trigger source. At *RST this value is set to 0 seconds.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Select the source from which the power supply accepts a trigger: BUS (software) or IMMediate (internal). At *RST the bus trigger source is selected. Query returns 'BUS' or 'IMM'.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Generate a trigger to the trigger subsystem when bus triggering is selected (TRIG:SOUR BUS). Has the same effect as the IEEE-488 Group Execute Trigger (GET). For RS-232 operation, send SYST:REM first.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Turn the front-panel display off or on. When off, outputs are not sent to the display and all annunciators are disabled except ERROR. The display turns on automatically when returning to local mode. Query returns '0' (OFF) or '1' (ON).

commands.name: display_text
commands.description: Display a message on the front panel (up to 12 characters; additional characters are truncated). Commas, periods and semicolons share a display space with the preceding character. Query returns the message as a quoted string.

parameters.name: string
parameters.range: quoted string, up to 12 displayed characters

commands.name: display_text_clear
commands.description: Clear the message displayed on the front panel.

commands.name: output_state
commands.description: Enable or disable the outputs of the power supply. When disabled the voltage is 0 V and the current is 20 mA. At *RST the output state is OFF. Query returns '0' (OFF) or '1' (ON).

commands.name: output_relay
commands.description: Set the state of two TTL signals on the RS-232 connector (pins 1 and 9) intended for an external relay/relay driver. When ON, pin 1 is high (4.5 V) and pin 9 is low (0.5 V); reversed when OFF. At *RST the relay state is OFF. Requires installing two internal jumpers. Query returns the TTL relay logic state.

commands.name: system_beep
commands.description: Issue a single beep immediately.

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Query the power supply's error queue. Up to 20 errors are stored and retrieved in FIFO order. Each error is returned as a number and quoted message string. If no errors have occurred, returns +0,"No error". If more than 20 errors occur, the most recent is replaced with -350,"Queue overflow". The queue is cleared at power-off or by *CLS.

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Query the SCPI version implemented. Returns a string of the form YYYY.V (year and version number for that year, e.g. 1996.0).

commands.name: system_local
commands.description: Place the power supply in local mode during RS-232 operation. All front-panel keys are fully functional.

commands.name: system_remote
commands.description: Place the power supply in remote mode for RS-232 operation. All front-panel keys except Local are disabled. Send this before transferring data over RS-232.

commands.name: system_rwlock
commands.description: Place the power supply in remote mode for RS-232 operation with all front-panel keys disabled, including the Local key. Same as SYST:REM but locks out Local.

commands.name: cal_count
commands.description: Query the number of times the power supply has been calibrated. The value increments by one per calibration point, so a complete calibration increases the count by 5.

commands.name: cal_current_data
commands.description: Enter a current value read from an external meter (calibration must be unsecured and output ON). Select the calibration level first (CAL:CURR:LEV MIN, then MID, then MAX); three successive values must be entered, after which new constants are stored in non-volatile memory.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: current value measured by external meter

commands.name: cal_current_level
commands.description: Set the power supply to a current calibration point to be entered with CAL:CURR (calibration unsecured, output ON). Three points must be entered, with the low-end point (MIN) selected and entered first.

commands.name: cal_current_protection
commands.description: Calibrate the overcurrent protection circuit (takes about 10 seconds). Calibration must be unsecured and the output shorted; current calibration must precede this command. The new overcurrent constant is stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: cal_dac_error
commands.description: Correct the differential nonlinearity error of the internal DAC without an external meter. Must be sent before calibrating the voltage; takes about 30 seconds.

commands.name: cal_secure_code
commands.description: Enter a new calibration security code. First unsecure with the old code, then enter the new code. The code may contain up to 12 characters and the first character must be a letter.

parameters.name: code
parameters.range: up to 12 characters, first must be a letter

commands.name: cal_secure_state
commands.description: Unsecure or secure the power supply for calibration using the security code (up to 12 characters). Query returns '0' (OFF/unsecured) or '1' (ON/secured).

parameters.name: code
parameters.range: calibration security code, up to 12 characters

commands.name: cal_string
commands.description: Record calibration information (e.g. last/next calibration date or serial number) in up to 40 characters. The power supply should be unsecured before sending. Query returns the message as a quoted string.

commands.name: cal_voltage_data
commands.description: Enter a voltage value read from an external meter (calibration must be unsecured and output ON). Select the calibration level first (CAL:VOLT:LEV MIN, then MID, then MAX); three successive values must be entered, after which new constants are stored in non-volatile memory.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: voltage value measured by external meter

commands.name: cal_voltage_level
commands.description: Set the power supply to a voltage calibration point to be entered with CAL:VOLT (calibration unsecured, output ON). Three points must be entered, with the low-end point (MIN) selected and entered first.

commands.name: cal_voltage_protection
commands.description: Calibrate the overvoltage protection circuit (takes about 10 seconds). Calibration must be unsecured and the output opened; voltage calibration must precede this command. The new overvoltage constant is stored in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition
commands.description: Query the Questionable Status condition register, which reports voltage/current regulation, overtemperature, and OVP/OCP trip conditions. Returns a decimal value equal to the binary-weighted sum of the set bits.

commands.name: status_questionable_event
commands.description: Query the Questionable Status event register. Bit 0 = constant current mode, bit 1 = constant voltage mode, bit 4 = overtemperature (fan fault), bit 9 = overvoltage protection tripped, bit 10 = overcurrent protection tripped. Returns the binary-weighted sum of set bits and clears the register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Set or query the enable register for the Questionable Status register. Write a decimal value equal to the binary-weighted sum of the bits to enable. STAT:QUES:ENAB 0 clears the enable register.

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Read the power supply identification string: manufacturer, model number, a field always '0', and a three-part revision code. Example: HEWLETT-PACKARD,E3633A,0,X.X-X.X-X.X

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset the power supply to its power-on state (output OFF; voltage 0 V; current to default; OVP/OCP ON; trigger source BUS; delay 0; low voltage range selected). Does not clear the error queue.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Perform a complete self-test. Returns '0' if the self-test passes or '1' (or any non-zero value) if it fails; a failure also generates an error message with additional information.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Store the present state of the power supply to one of three non-volatile memory locations (1, 2 or 3). Remembers CURR, CURR:STEP, CURR:TRIG, CURR:PROT, CURR:PROT:STAT, DISP, OUTP, OUTP:REL, TRIG:DEL, TRIG:SOUR, VOLT, VOLT:STEP, VOLT:TRIG, VOLT:PROT, VOLT:PROT:STAT and VOLT:RANG.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recall a previously stored state from one of three non-volatile memory locations (1, 2 or 3). Use the same location used previously to store the state.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear the event registers in all register groups and the error queue.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Set or query the enable register for the Standard Event register. Write a decimal value equal to the binary-weighted sum of the bits to enable.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Query the Standard Event register and clear it. Returns the binary-weighted sum of set bits (bit 0 OPC, 2 QYE, 3 DDE, 4 EXE, 5 CME, 7 PON).

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Set the OPC bit (bit 0) in the Standard Event register when all pending operations complete. The query form returns '1' to the output buffer when the operation is complete.

commands.name: power_on_status_clear
commands.description: Enable (1) or disable (0) clearing of the Standard Event enable register and Status Byte enable register at power-on. Query returns the present setting.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Set or query the Status Byte enable register (Request Service). Write a decimal value equal to the binary-weighted sum of the bits to enable. *SRE 0 clears the register.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Query the Status Byte summary register. Returns the binary-weighted sum of set bits (bit 3 QUES, 4 MAV, 5 ESB, 6 RQS). The register is not latched.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Wait for all pending operations to complete before executing any additional commands. Useful for synchronization with bus triggering.
